sourceMovie.title: Locked
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Locked works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Goodnight Mommy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Locked compelling — the horror, the thriller — shows up in Goodnight Mommy (2022) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: The Jester
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The horror energy of Locked is alive in The Jester (2023), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Whisper
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Whisper (2007) operates in the same horror as Locked, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The dark payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Bloodline
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Bloodline (2019) belongs to the same conversation as Locked — both anchor their horror in thriller emotion that doesn't let go. The dark undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Vicious
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Locked's horror stayed with you, Vicious (2025) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The thriller through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Succubus
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Locked excels in horror, Succubus (2024) doubles down on thriller to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: You'll Never Find Me
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: You'll Never Find Me (2024) is the natural next step after Locked: same horror DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and thriller elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Play Dead
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Locked who respond to its horror will find Play Dead (2022) immediately familiar. Both films share a thriller sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Eradication
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Locked fans keep landing on Eradication (2022) — the horror is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Candy Land
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Candy Land draws from the same horror well as Locked, channeling thriller in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2023 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

faq.question: What should I watch after Locked?
faq.answer: Try Goodnight Mommy, The Jester, Whisper—each shares DNA with Locked while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Locked?
faq.answer: Locked (2025) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Locked worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
